evals.prompt: I have a React component library. DESIGN.md shows Figma mockups for a Button (blue background, white text, 12px padding). I implemented it as a Tailwind Button. How do I validate design fidelity?
evals.expected_output: Phase 1: Extract reference from DESIGN.md (Button spec: Tailwind 'bg-blue-600 text-white px-3 py-2', expected color #2563EB, text Helvetica 12px). Save to reference/button-spec.md. Phase 2: Render live button in a test page at design viewport (e.g., 1440px), screenshot to implementation/button-screenshot.png. Extract computed styles (bg color, text color, padding) via browser tools. Phase 3: Compare: Check if computed background color matches #2563EB (PASS/FAIL), text color matches white (PASS/FAIL), padding matches 12px (PASS/FAIL). Score by category (Colors 100%, Typography 95%, etc.). Overall score determines verdict. If score >85, PASS and Button is production-ready.

evals.prompt: I have both a DESIGN.md and a Stitch project for the same UI. They show slightly different colors. Which should I use as the source of truth?
evals.expected_output: Stitch is the preferred source (line 36: 'preferred'). Stitch is more up-to-date and auditable. Use Stitch as primary reference, document in reference/source.md. If Stitch colors differ from DESIGN.md, that indicates DESIGN.md is stale. After validation, update DESIGN.md to match Stitch to keep specs in sync. Document the discrepancy in the report under 'Deviations Found'.
